Description: Unique identifier for each participant.
Sequential identifier of trials within the experiment.
Experimental version (1 or 2), indicating the initial response mapping rule.
Experimental phase (e.g., training, experiment).
Block number within the experiment.
Sequential index of experimental segments, including training and main blocks.
The word presented on the screen.
Vertical position of the word in the semantic space (e.g., top, bottom).
Assigned correct response location (top or bottom) based on the current instruction mapping.
Time in milliseconds from stimulus onset to movement initiation.
Time in milliseconds from stimulus onset to movement completion.
Actual response location where the participant moved the word (top or bottom).
Whether the response matched the assigned condition (1 = correct, 0 = incorrect).
Ground-truth semantic category of the word (abstract or concrete).
Participant’s age in years.
Self-reported gender of the participant.
Self-reported handedness (e.g., right-handed, left-handed).
Input device used (e.g., computer mouse, trackpad).
Identifier of the stimulus item.
Response rule active during the trial (mapping between concreteness category and response location).
Participant’s inferred semantic judgment (abstract or concrete), reconstructed from the response and instruction mapping.
